ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Deerling,' National Pokedex no. 585, small fawn with flower on head in snowy forest — matches Deerling canonical design. The pink-blossom head matches the Spring Form, though the snowy setting is inconsistent with spring; the art may show Spring Form in an off-season environment, or it is the Winter Form with a pink head (Winter Form is canonically brown). Flagging as verify with qualification: the art reads as Spring Form coloring (pink blossom) in a winter setting — an artistic choice common in the TCG. IP is unambiguously Deerling regardless of which form is depicted.

trivia_bullets.text: Deerling is National Pokedex no. 585, a Normal/Grass type introduced in Generation V (Black and White, 2010). It has four seasonal forms — Spring, Summer, Autumn, and Winter — that change its fur color (pink, green, orange, brown respectively) and scent. Originally, form changed based on the Nintendo DS internal clock season setting; from Generation VI onward, only the Spring Form could be encountered in the wild in most games, with other forms obtainable via transfer or Pokemon GO. [WebSearch: Bulbapedia snippet]

Deerling's scent changes with its form: delicate and sweet in spring, grassy in summer, softly sweet in autumn, and no scent at all in winter. Its flavor text encodes its role as a natural calendar: 'The turning of the seasons changes the color and scent of this Pokemon's fur. People use it to mark the seasons.' [PokemonTCG.io: swsh6-11]
'The turning of the seasons changes the color and scent of this Pokemon's fur. People use it to mark the seasons.' [PokemonTCG.io: swsh6-11]

The vision-pass notes pink-blossom head coloring (consistent with Spring Form) against a snowy winter background — an artistic juxtaposition. The Chilling Reign set's winter/cold theme may explain the snowy setting being applied to what appears to be a Spring-colored Deerling.
